div.head: The Ambassador in Italy (Child) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 203. Department’s 119, August 11, 6 p.m.;Not printed; see telegram no. 87, Aug. 11, to the Ambassador in Japan, p. 910. 128, August 28, 3 p.m.;Not printed; see telegram no. 95, Aug. 28, to the Ambassador in Japan, p. 913. and 147, October 13, 7 p.m.See telegram no. 311, Oct. 13, to the Ambassador in France, with instruction to repeat to Rome as no. 147, p. 920. Ministry of Foreign Affairs states that the Italian Government adheres to the proposals contained in the memorandum of the first instance (Department’s 119, August 11, 6 p.m.) for the cessation of the Inter-Allied Committee and of the technical office instituted by the Inter-Allied railway agreement of January, 1919, and to the common action between the Powers interested in regard to the control and operation of the Oriental Trans-Siberian Railway.
